sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation and synthetic-data generation expand training variation through transformations, simulation, model generation, or controlled perturbation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation and Chapters 20 on generative models; GAN, VAE, diffusion, and flow-matching papers for model-based generation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Augmentation via transformations and synthetic/model-generated data are supported across the cited sources, though simulation and controlled perturbation are generalized.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation is to connect Datasets, sampling & splits, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data to downstream capabilities including Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation, Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), Domain randomization & simulation curricula.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation and generalization context; AIMA learning, robotics, and simulated-environment topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Datasets, augmentation, and robustness/generalization links are supported. Multimodal/streaming data, RLVR, and domain-randomization curricula are not directly supported by the packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Robustness, distribution shift & OOD evaluation
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation and Chapter 5/7 generalization and regularization context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Augmentation is supported as a regularization/generalization technique. Distribution shift and OOD evaluation are not directly evidenced in the cited source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR)
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA reinforcement learning and simulated environments/robotics topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected packet sources do not support the modern RLVR-specific downstream claim.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Domain randomization & simulation curricula
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA robotics, reinforcement learning, and simulation-adjacent planning/control topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Simulation and curricula are broadly relevant to AI/RL, but domain randomization is not directly supported by the cited packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapters 5, 7, and 11 on data, augmentation, hyperparameters, and methodology; PML data and preprocessing topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Data and hyperparameter sensitivity are supported. Compute budget, random variation, evaluation contamination, and multimodal/simulated/streaming dependencies are broader than cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation uses Datasets, sampling & splits as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation; PML data and preprocessing topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Augmentation operates on or supplements datasets, so the dependency is defensible.

sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation uses Multimodal, simulated & streaming data as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, image/text data and augmentation/generative-model contexts; AIMA robotics/simulation-adjacent topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Multimodal and simulated data can be augmentation sources, but streaming data is not directly supported and the dependency is not always necessary.

sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation uses Equivariance, invariance & geometric priors as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 7.4 dataset augmentation and Chapter 9 convolution/equivariance context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Transformation-based augmentation often encodes invariances; exact dependency wording is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Data augmentation & synthetic-data generation uses Generative modeling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning, Chapter 20 generative models; GAN, VAE, diffusion, and flow-matching papers as model-generation sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: Synthetic-data generation can use generative models; augmentation more broadly need not.

edge_reviews.notes: Data augmentation operates on datasets and sampled examples; core strength is defensible.
Simulated/multimodal data are plausible sources, but not a necessary core dependency and streaming data is not directly evidenced.
Transformation-based augmentation often exploits invariance assumptions; common strength is defensible.
Generative models support synthetic-data generation; common strength is appropriate because not all augmentation is generative.
